threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Underground'—everyone here knows it's a London thing, not a tourist thing.
Saw a guy in a suit on the 59 last night, nodding off with a pizza box in his lap. When he jolted awake, he nearly sent it flying!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I spotted a street musician playing classical opera on a trumpet while a group of kids danced around him, totally capturing the Neukölln spirit.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see a crowd waiting at a tram stop while the bus zips by empty. It's like a public transport dance-off every morning.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only real locals know to carry an umbrella in November but leave it at home in February. The weather's as moody as our coffee preferences.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading commuters at the Civic Center station, pausing only for the deafening train horn. Everyone just kept scrolling on their phones.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all hip cafes and bars, but the real gems are the little bakeries that open at dawn – best croissants you’ll ever have.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still carry a map instead of checking their phone. You can see the confusion when they stare at the street signs with the wrong pronunciations.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Dalston Junction' instead of just 'the Junction'. Old hands know better than to be so formal around here.
Caught a glimpse of a guy serenading a pigeon on the 35 last night. The bird didn't seem impressed, but he carried on like it was sold-out Wembley.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the old lady down the street offered me her homemade pickles while gossiping about the latest BVG strike. That's when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a street vendor selling currywurst next to an open-air techno party at 11 AM on a Sunday. That’s a Sunday ritual!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re locals when they know to check BART status before leaving—everyone else just assumes it’ll run on time. Good luck with that.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 Fulton yesterday, and a guy serenaded us with a ukulele. Only in the Tenderloin, right? At least he made the delay bearable.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals hit the bars late, but many of us are home by 10 for family dinner. The nightlife is just a slice of Gràcia life.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still look up at the buildings, mesmerized by the stained glass, while locals barely notice.
